nodes.label: Sprite Game Asset Tool Design Spec
nodes.norm_label: sprite game asset tool design spec

nodes.label: Two-Tier Local Architecture
nodes.rationale: Hard line between AI layer (non-deterministic Gemini) and pipeline layer (deterministic pure Python). Pipeline never calls Gemini; only transforms images.

nodes.label: Pipeline Layer
nodes.rationale: Pure Image→Image functions testable with fixed input PNGs; no network, no I/O, no config reads.

nodes.label: Gemini Client Isolation
nodes.rationale: gemini_client.py is the only module importing the Gemini SDK so swapping AI provider later touches one file.

nodes.label: Stage 1 Static Sprite
nodes.rationale: Foundation milestone: describe → clean sprite → export; shippable on its own.

nodes.label: Stage 2 Animation
nodes.rationale: Motion is the moat; static alone is too small. Animated sprite → sheet + atlas with regenerate cleanup.

nodes.label: Base-Anchored Editing
nodes.rationale: Every frame passes the original base sprite plus a per-frame prompt; anchoring to one reference each call beats chaining frame→frame which drifts.

nodes.label: Shared Bounding Box Alignment
nodes.rationale: One bounding box covering every frame so the character does not jitter or resize between frames; deterministic and testable.

nodes.label: Regenerate-Per-Frame Escape Hatch
nodes.rationale: FrameStrip lets users delete and regenerate a single bad frame; pragmatic answer to Gemini not being frame-perfect.

nodes.label: Preset Action Library
nodes.rationale: Reliable core idle/walk/run; best-effort attack/jump. Adding a preset = adding table rows, no new code.

nodes.label: Filesystem Project Storage
nodes.rationale: Plain filesystem, no database; each project is ./projects/<uuid>/ with project.json manifest.

nodes.label: Deterministic vs Non-Deterministic Testing Split
nodes.rationale: Everything deterministic tested exhaustively and for free; the single non-deterministic seam is mocked in CI and smoke-tested by hand.

nodes.label: Configurable Gemini Model Selection
nodes.rationale: GEMINI_MODEL_EDIT must stay on full Flash Image because Lite is not optimized for multiple reference inputs; Stage 1 can optionally use Lite as a cost lever.

nodes.label: Python Imaging Stack Tradeoff
nodes.rationale: Two-language split means two dev servers vs all-JS Next.js, but Python Pillow/NumPy/rembg imaging ecosystem is worth it for a local personal tool.

nodes.label: Locked Guiding Decisions
nodes.rationale: Learning/personal tool; Gemini + local post-processing; animated sprite in 2 stages; pixel and hi-res; React+Vite and FastAPI; small preset library.

nodes.label: Sprite Game Asset Tool Implementation Plan
nodes.norm_label: sprite game asset tool implementation plan

nodes.label: TDD Implementation Approach
nodes.rationale: Deterministic pipeline tested exhaustively against committed fixtures; Gemini seam mocked in CI and smoke-tested by hand.

nodes.label: Pipeline Purity Convention
nodes.rationale: Every pipeline/* function is Image→Image or [Image]→Image with no network, disk, or config reads; primary test surface.

nodes.label: Seam Dependency Injection
nodes.rationale: rembg and Gemini SDK client are injected so tests substitute fakes.

nodes.label: google-genai SDK Call Shape
nodes.rationale: No Interactions API; both generation and editing use client.models.generate_content with response_modalities IMAGE, isolated to gemini_client.py.

nodes.label: Global Region Model Risk Resolution
nodes.rationale: gemini-3.1-flash-image 404s at us-central1; Gemini 3.x image models serve from global endpoint. Default region now global.

nodes.label: Partial Animation Failure Tolerance
nodes.rationale: One failed frame returns status failed with placeholder while other frames succeed; successful frames never lost.

nodes.norm_label: application configuration.  auth model: vertex ai / google agent platform. prefe

nodes.label: Small deterministic fallback so edit availability is independently probed.
nodes.norm_label: small deterministic fallback so edit availability is independently probed.

nodes.label: MCP tools/list Audit Distinction
nodes.rationale: When auditing an MCP-backed product, distinguish tools exported directly by the MCP server from tools available internally to remote agents; absence from tools/list does not prove the product lacks that capability.

nodes.label: Deterministic Image Pipeline
nodes.rationale: Background removal, pixel-art quantization, sprite-sheet packing, and atlas metadata are handled deterministically in Python after generative models produce frames.

nodes.label: sprite-mcp Local MCP Server
nodes.rationale: Stdio MCP server shares SpriteService with FastAPI; exposes list_projects, get_project, enhance_prompt, generate_sprite, animate, regenerate_frame, export_sheet; no delete tool; no arbitrary reference-image paths; scoped to PROJECTS_DIR.

nodes.label: Vertex AI Gemini Authentication
nodes.rationale: Authenticates via service-account JSON key (GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS), not GEMINI_API_KEY; Gemini 3.x image models require the global endpoint.

nodes.label: Hyperagent Experimental Provider
nodes.rationale: Shown as Experimental but disabled until agent-mediated image path is validated.

nodes.label: Live Gemini Model Validation
nodes.rationale: Manual disposable acceptance check outside pytest/CI; billable live calls; no retries; redacts project ID; writes to scripts/live_validation_out/.

nodes.label: Supported Whole-App Location global
nodes.rationale: Image models are global-only; text may use us/eu for PayGo, but us/eu makes the configured image model unsupported; whole-app intersection is therefore global.

nodes.label: Manual Output-Quality Rubric
nodes.rationale: Score 0/1/2 per criterion; pass requires mean >= 1.5 and no critical criterion scored 0; covers prompt enhancement, base sprite, and edited-frame criteria.

nodes.label: Opt-in Safety Block Probe
nodes.rationale: Opt-in so normal smoke never sends policy-boundary content; expects provider safety block and discards returned image data.

nodes.label: Burst Quota Recovery Finding
nodes.rationale: 2026-07-21 run hit burst-quota after two immediate image edits; with 15s quota cooldown and pose-reference inputs, four-frame walk completed 4/4 in 146.6s — validates recovery, not production capacity.

nodes.label: Azure GPT Image 2 Acceptance Snapshot
nodes.rationale: Same manual rubric; identity and pose as two ordered image[] inputs; /openai/v1/images/edits?api-version=preview; three concurrent edits; serial rembg/align/quantize.

nodes.label: Hyperagent Not Listed as Supported
nodes.rationale: Authenticated MCP exports agent/thread orchestration but no direct image tool; no image-enabled agent from list_agents; remains experimental until dedicated sprite agent path is validated.

nodes.label: MCP After Transport-Neutral Service Extraction
nodes.rationale: MCP fits after route workflows are extracted into transport-neutral services shared by FastAPI and MCP, with project locking and atomic persistence.

nodes.label: Project Continuity Creative Controls MCP Roadmap
nodes.norm_label: project continuity creative controls mcp roadmap

nodes.label: ProjectStore Persistence Hub
nodes.rationale: Filesystem folders and project.json remain source of truth; FastAPI, React, and MCP become adapters around shared domain and application services.

nodes.label: SpriteService Application Boundary
nodes.rationale: Framework-neutral service accepting ProjectStore, GeminiClient, and remover; domain values not Request/UploadFile/HTTPException/MCP context; MCP tools call service directly never HTTP.

nodes.label: GeminiClient
nodes.rationale: Owns model selection, retry, timeout, and error classification; correct home for text-only enhancement primitive with separately configurable GEMINI_MODEL_TEXT.

nodes.label: Direction Enum
nodes.rationale: Eight directions; side-scroller accepts only left/right; top-down/2.5D accepts all eight; existing manifests default to side_scroller + left.

nodes.label: Opt-in Prompt Enhancement
nodes.rationale: Explicit preview/edit/accept; never hidden enhancement on generate; persist prompt, enhanced_prompt, prompt_source; raw remains available on enhancer failure.

nodes.label: FastMCP Stdio Server
nodes.rationale: Stable MCP Python SDK v1 FastMCP (mcp>=1.12,<2); stdio; diagnostics on stderr; lifespan-shared store/client/service; no delete tools or reference-image paths in v1.

nodes.label: Eight Explicit Mirrored Gait Phases
nodes.rationale: Give walk animations eight explicit mirrored gait phases and require visible limb/silhouette changes in every edit prompt.

nodes.label: Left/Right Directional Adherence Gap
nodes.rationale: Open item: improve or deterministically normalize left/right directional adherence; accepted-quality gate treats wrong facing as critical.

hyperedges.label: Shared SpriteService HTTP and MCP Adapters
